score_note: Correctly explains dominance and the cell/room/door cardinality refinements.
Correctly connects minimum-image characterization to the later cardinality bound.
Correctly distinguishes point insertion and index insertion, but omits door-room incidence counting.
Correctly distinguishes image, missing color, and typed missing color, but does not explain the parity-counting role.
Recognizes that room_of_colorful supplies the room/cardinality prerequisite, but misses nonempty and the cardinality sandwich.
Identifies typed door-room incidences and parity lemmas, but omits the non-colorful room side and final odd colorful-room count.
Correctly explains singleton outside-door contribution and odd cardinality.
Recognizes parity-to-existence at a high level, but misses the exact odd/even incidence equation.
States colorful existence, but gives the wrong reason for Inhabited I and misses default as the chosen type.
Correctly connects Fcolor, grid levels, Scarf/colorful rooms, room_seq, and room_point_seq.
Recognizes finite-image subsequence extraction, but treats the stabilized object as a color/grid value rather than the set C.
Explains outside-C coordinates tending to zero, but misses the coordinate inequality mechanism for fixedness.
Mentions compactness and vanishing diameter, but misses transfer of inequalities from room witnesses to the limit.
States the limiting coordinate inequality, but omits colored witnesses and the continuity/diameter transfer.
Gives the grid/subsequence/diameter strategy, but does not explicitly identify the Scarf step or continuity transfer.
Explains deficit/tPush/pushTowardsZ generally, but misses positive blockSum as the normalization denominator issue.
Identifies projection after embedding as a retraction, but does not explain the lifted-map fixed-point conversion.
Correctly summarizes f_lifted, continuity, Brouwer, projection back, and project_embed_id.
Identifies mixedS as product of simplices, but omits expected payoff and unilateral-deviation condition.
Connects nash_map continuity to Brouwer fixed points, but misses payoff improvement and normalization.
Connects Brouwer.mixedGame to a nash_map fixed point, but misses pure-deviation contradiction and mixed_g_linear.
Correctly explains two incident rooms, fiber size two, and evenness of the internal-door side.
Correctly explains same-missing-type versus colorful partition for typed incidences.
Mentions a size-two fiber and even contribution, but omits the two nearly-colorful doors mechanism.
Correctly explains TT finite grids, division by l, and indexed order for Scarf.
Recognizes coordinate bounds and convergence use, but misstates size_bound_in/out and misses the outside-C estimate.
Explains stabilizing a constant color set along a subsequence, but misses outside-C and in-C coordinate inequalities.
Mentions coordinate and sum constraints, but gives an imprecise outside-C/equality argument and omits extensionality.
Correctly explains flat versus block indexing and inverse maps, but misses the sum-transformation role.
Identifies blockWeight scaling and big-simplex compatibility, but misses sum-one detail and project_embed_id recovery.
Correctly explains division by blockSum, positive denominator, and continuity through push/blockSum composition.
Incorrectly says the case split is unnecessary and misses the zero-deficit argument when tPush = 0.
Correctly explains mixed_g_linear as weighted pure-deviation expansion for mixed deviations.
Explains transport of fixed points through simplex equivalences, but misses Fintype.equivFin/ProductSimplices specifics.
Mentions g_function, nonnegativity, sum lower bound, and normalization, but does not clearly prove sum-one simplex membership.
Gives an invalid contradiction from 1 < sum_g versus 1 <= sum_g and misses the fixed-point self-division mechanism.
